positioning.thesis: FluencyTracr should measure and govern the enterprise shift from ad hoc AI assistance to repeatable, governed, agentic, outcome-linked work. ROI is the final claim layer after evidence, controls, outcome linkage, and finance approval.
core_product_questions: What work is AI participating in?
What maturity stage is that work at?
What outcomes are linked or missing?
What evidence would upgrade those claims?

journey_steps.label: Survey opportunity
journey_steps.what_it_shows: Aggregate user-reported friction points indicate where AI assistance may create value, before product telemetry or outcome linkage exists.
journey_steps.safe_claim: Survey evidence identifies directional value opportunities for follow-up instrumentation.
blocked_claims: Do not claim measured ROI from survey opportunity alone.
next_upgrade_actions: Attach aggregate product telemetry to the surveyed workflow categories.

journey_steps.label: Search and chat telemetry
journey_steps.what_it_shows: Aggregate search, chat, and AI Answers activity shows where AI is repeatedly participating in common work patterns.
evidence_present: No raw prompt, response, or query content
journey_steps.safe_claim: Product telemetry shows repeated AI assistance in covered search and assistant workflows.
blocked_claims: Do not claim time savings or business value without outcome evidence.
Do not reconstruct raw work content.
next_upgrade_actions: Group aggregate events into repeatable work patterns and attach claim readiness.

journey_steps.label: Repeatable work patterns
journey_steps.what_it_shows: Aggregate AI activity can be represented as work patterns such as find, understand, summarize, analyze, and troubleshoot instead of isolated feature usage.
evidence_present: Directional survey opportunity aligned to observed product telemetry
journey_steps.safe_claim: Evidence shows AI participation in repeatable work patterns across covered surfaces.
blocked_claims: Do not rank teams or managers by pattern activity.
next_upgrade_actions: Track reusable outputs from skills, artifacts, and workflow templates.

journey_steps.label: Skills and artifacts
journey_steps.what_it_shows: Reusable skills and artifacts indicate that AI-supported work is moving from one-off assistance into repeatable expertise.
journey_steps.safe_claim: Evidence supports reusable AI-assisted expertise for covered workflow outputs.
blocked_claims: Do not expose file contents or generated artifact contents.
Do not claim revenue or cost impact from artifact counts alone.
next_upgrade_actions: Connect reusable outputs to workflow runs and external process metrics.

journey_steps.label: Agentic execution
journey_steps.what_it_shows: Agent and API-supported workflows show AI participating in multi-step execution, not just single-turn assistance.
evidence_present: Generated output counts without raw content
journey_steps.safe_claim: Internal evidence shows covered agentic workflows are executing repeatable multi-step work.
blocked_claims: Do not claim autonomous business impact without governed controls and outcome linkage.
next_upgrade_actions: Attach control evidence and approval coverage for governed actions.

journey_steps.label: Governed action
journey_steps.what_it_shows: Governed actions add reviewable control evidence, approval coverage, and safe-path constraints before stronger claims are allowed.
evidence_present: Control coverage for covered action classes
journey_steps.safe_claim: Internal evidence supports governed execution for covered action classes.
blocked_claims: Do not claim causal risk reduction or financial impact from action logs alone.
Do not hide suppressed or unreportable values.
next_upgrade_actions: Link governed workflows to aggregate external outcome metrics.

journey_steps.label: Outcome-linked evidence
journey_steps.what_it_shows: Aggregate AI work evidence is linked to external systems of record such as customer success, support, engineering, security, finance, and HR metrics.
evidence_present: Privacy boundary and minimum sample size
journey_steps.safe_claim: Covered evidence supports a contribution story between AI-assisted work and linked business outcomes, with attribution caveats.
blocked_claims: Do not claim causality unless the attribution design supports it.
Do not claim payback before finance approval.
next_upgrade_actions: Submit the outcome linkage and assumptions for Finance and Data Science review.

journey_steps.label: Finance-approved value claim
journey_steps.what_it_shows: Only after governed workflow evidence, linked outcomes, and finance-approved assumptions exist can FluencyTracr produce bounded value language.
journey_steps.safe_claim: Finance-reviewed evidence supports a bounded value claim for governed agentic business reporting workflows.
blocked_claims: Do not generalize the value claim to all AI work.
Do not remove approved assumptions or caveats from the report-out.
next_upgrade_actions: Monitor drift in workflow coverage, control evidence, outcome linkage, and financial assumptions.

strongest_claim.safe_claim_language: Finance-reviewed evidence supports a bounded value claim for governed agentic business reporting workflows.
caveats: Finance approval is scoped to the synthetic workflow and approved assumptions.
Do not generalize this value claim to all agentic work.

blocked_stronger_claims: Do not generalize this value claim to all agentic work.
Do not generalize this value claim beyond the approved workflow, window, and financial assumptions.
upgrade_actions: Monitor drift in reporting workflow coverage, control evidence, and financial assumptions.
Revalidate finance approval when pricing, recapture, or workflow scope changes.
governance_boundaries: No raw prompts, raw responses, transcripts, query text, tool payloads, or file contents.
No direct identifiers, manager views, team rankings, or productivity scoring.
ROI is downstream of evidence, controls, outcomes, and finance approval.
